A script to check for undefined global variable definitions of pins in JavaScript files using jshint.

# Purpose
This script is a Bash executable that performs a static code analysis on JavaScript files located in the `js` directory. It uses `jshint`, a tool for detecting errors and potential problems in JavaScript code, with a configuration specified in `tools/jshint.conf`. The script concatenates all JavaScript files in the directory, runs them through `jshint`, and filters the output for instances of "not defined" errors using `grep`. If any such errors are found, the script exits with a status code of 1, indicating a failure; otherwise, it exits with a status code of 0, indicating success. This script is useful for ensuring that all global variable definitions are correctly handled in the JavaScript files.
